rag_rules: Fix unresolved references in AGREE contracts by ensuring all identifiers are visible in the current scope or declared as helpers.
Move implementation-level bindings from component type annexes to implementation annexes to avoid scope errors.
Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers in AGREE code that are not present in the model's configured dependencies.
generic_rules: When AGREE diagnostics report '<error>' types, trace and resolve the first unresolved identifier on the same line.
Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE contracts to prevent reference errors.
Validate that AGREE scalar type names match supported types: int, real, bool.
Ensure AGREE contracts are placed in the correct AADL scope: type annexes for component-level contracts, implementation annexes for bindings.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assumptions and guarantees referencing ports must be in valid component type scopes.
Implementation-level bindings must be in implementation annexes after relevant subcomponents.
AGREE expressions must resolve to valid boolean types for assume/guarantee statements.
anti_patterns: Do not add new architecture elements to fix AGREE reference errors; instead, correct or remove invalid clauses.
Do not place implementation-level references in component type annexes; move them to implementation annexes.
Do not use invalid scalar type names in AGREE declarations; use only int, real, bool.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about property associations
AGREE validator diagnostics about missing annex placement
